get_workflow_guide.name: get_workflow_guide
get_workflow_guide.description: Get workflow guides for using Context Engine tools effectively. Call this at the start of a session to understand which tools to use for common workflows like investigating services, responding to incidents, assessing blast radius, managing dependencies, and exploring architecture. Returns a comprehensive guide covering all major workflows with step-by-step instructions.

workflow.description: Optional filter to a specific workflow: "investigate", "incident", "dependency", "architecture", "blast-radius", "search". If omitted, returns all workflow guides.

list_upgrades.name: list_upgrades
list_upgrades.description: List all upgrade events discovered in a repository. Returns package name, versions, whether code changes were required, commit info, and upgrade source. Run the upgrade-history-analyzer agent first to populate this data.

ecosystem.description: Filter by ecosystem (npm, maven, pypi, go, rust)

limit.description: Maximum number of upgrades to return (default 50)

get_upgrades_without_code_changes.name: get_upgrades_without_code_changes
get_upgrades_without_code_changes.description: Get upgrade events that were simple version bumps with no code changes. These are clean upgrades where only manifest/lock files changed. Useful for identifying dependencies that are safe to auto-merge. Run the upgrade-history-analyzer agent first to populate this data.

ecosystem.description: Filter by ecosystem (npm, maven, pypi, go, rust)

limit.description: Maximum number of upgrades to return (default 30)

get_upgrades_with_code_changes.name: get_upgrades_with_code_changes
get_upgrades_with_code_changes.description: Get upgrade events that required code changes beyond the manifest file. These are upgrades that caused friction - requiring migrations, API updates, or breaking change fixes. Useful for identifying problematic dependencies. Run the upgrade-history-analyzer agent first to populate this data.

ecosystem.description: Filter by ecosystem (npm, maven, pypi, go, rust)

limit.description: Maximum number of upgrades to return (default 30)

get_upgrade_summary.name: get_upgrade_summary
get_upgrade_summary.description: Get a summary of upgrade analysis for a repository. Returns statistics about total upgrades, breakdown by category (with/without code changes), top upgraded packages, and upgrade sources (dependabot, renovate, manual). Run the upgrade-history-analyzer agent first to populate this data.

repository.description: Repository name to get summary for (e.g., "owner/repo")

limit.description: Maximum number of snapshots to return (default 1, latest)

get_package_upgrade_history.name: get_package_upgrade_history
get_package_upgrade_history.description:
Get the upgrade history for a specific package. Shows all version changes over time, whether each upgrade required code changes, and who performed them. Run the upgrade-history-analyzer agent first to populate this data.
PREFER: Use 'dependency_check' for complete package assessment including security status, upgrade history, and migration recommendations in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need only the raw upgrade event history, or when analyzing upgrade patterns across multiple packages.

limit.description: Maximum number of upgrades to return (default 20)

get_major_upgrades.name: get_major_upgrades
get_major_upgrades.description:
Get major version upgrades (e.g., 4.x to 5.x) that historically required code changes. These are the most impactful upgrades that often involve breaking changes and migrations. Run the upgrade-history-analyzer agent first to populate this data.
PREFER: Use 'dependency_check' for complete package assessment including major upgrade history, security status, and migration recommendations in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need to analyze major upgrades across multiple packages, or when researching upgrade patterns organization-wide.

ecosystem.description: Filter by ecosystem (npm, maven, pypi, go, rust)

limit.description: Maximum number of upgrades to return (default 20)

update_slack_message.name: update_slack_message
update_slack_message.description: Update an existing Slack message. Requires a connected and enabled Slack data source. Uses the Slack chat.update API.

channel.description: Slack channel ID containing the message (e.g., "C1234567890").

ts.description: Timestamp of the message to update.

text.description: The new message text. Supports Slack mrkdwn formatting.

post_slack_message.name: post_slack_message
post_slack_message.description: Post a message to a Slack channel. Requires a connected and enabled Slack data source. Uses the Slack chat.postMessage API. Supports threading via thread_ts parameter.

channel.description: Slack channel ID (e.g., "C1234567890") or channel name (e.g., "#general").

text.description: The message text to post. Supports Slack mrkdwn formatting.

thread_ts.description: Timestamp of the parent message to reply in a thread. Leave empty to post as a new message.

list_services.name: list_services
list_services.description: List all services in the knowledge graph with their metadata. Returns service name, team, tier, language, framework, and purpose. Use this to get an overview of all services or filter by team/tier/language.

team.description: Filter by team name (partial match supported). Example: "Commerce", "Platform"

repository.description: Filter by repository name (partial match supported).

limit.description: Maximum number of services to return (default 50, max 200).

get_service.name: get_service
get_service.description:
Get detailed information about a specific service including all its relationships. Returns service metadata, owning team, dependencies (packages, databases, services it calls), and dependents (services that call it).
PREFER: Use 'investigate_service' for comprehensive analysis that also includes documentation, business flows, and architectural context in a single call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the basic service info and relationships without the full investigation context, or when building custom queries.

serviceName.description: Service name to look up (e.g., "order-service", "payment-api"). Supports partial matching.

get_service_dependents.name: get_service_dependents
get_service_dependents.description:
Get all dependents of a service - what depends on it. Shows services that call it, services that subscribe to its topics, and packages that import it.
PREFER: Use 'blast_radius' for complete impact analysis including transitive dependents, affected flows, and teams to notify. Use 'investigate_service' if you also need the service's own dependencies.
USE THIS WHEN: You need only the inbound dependents (what calls this service), not the full impact analysis.

serviceName.description: Service name to analyze (e.g., "payment-service"). Supports partial matching.

relationshipType.description: Filter to specific relationship type: "CALLS_HTTP", "CALLS_GRPC", "SUBSCRIBES_TO". Leave empty for all dependent types.

get_service_dependencies.name: get_service_dependencies
get_service_dependencies.description:
Get all dependencies of a service - what it depends on. Shows services it calls (HTTP/gRPC), packages it uses, databases it connects to, and message topics it publishes/subscribes to.
PREFER: Use 'investigate_service' for comprehensive view including dependencies, dependents, documentation, and flows in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need only the outbound dependencies (what this service relies on), not the full service context.

serviceName.description: Service name to analyze (e.g., "order-service"). Supports partial matching.

relationshipType.description: Filter to specific relationship type: "CALLS_HTTP", "CALLS_GRPC", "USES_PACKAGE", "USES_DATABASE", "PUBLISHES_TO", "SUBSCRIBES_TO". Leave empty for all dependency types.

get_service_context.name: get_service_context
get_service_context.description:
Get comprehensive context for a service combining multiple data sources. Returns service info, team ownership, dependencies, dependents, code hotspots, recent incidents, relevant ADRs, and linked GitLab issues with acceptance criteria.
PREFER: Use 'investigate_service' for the most comprehensive service analysis including all context plus documentation and flow participation in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need a quick service overview without the full documentation and flow context, or when profiling multiple services.

serviceName.description: Service name to get context for (e.g., "order-service"). Supports partial matching.

update_servicenow_incident.name: update_servicenow_incident
update_servicenow_incident.description: Update an existing ServiceNow incident. Requires a connected and enabled ServiceNow data source. IMPORTANT: All fields in the request body are sent to ServiceNow — omitted optional parameters are sent as empty strings, which may clear those fields. Only call this tool with ALL fields you want to preserve on the record.

sys_id.description: The sys_id of the incident to update.

short_description.description: Updated brief summary of the incident.

assignment_group.description: Sys ID of the assignment group to reassign the incident to.

assigned_to.description: Sys ID of the user to assign the incident to.

close_code.description: Resolution code when resolving or closing (e.g., "Solved (Permanently)", "Solved (Work Around)", "Not Solved").

close_notes.description: Resolution notes describing how the incident was resolved.

list_servicenow_incidents.name: list_servicenow_incidents
list_servicenow_incidents.description: List or search ServiceNow incidents. Requires a connected and enabled ServiceNow data source. Supports filtering by state, priority, assignment group, and encoded query. Returns up to the specified limit of incidents.

state.description: Filter by incident state: "1" (New), "2" (In Progress), "3" (On Hold), "6" (Resolved), "7" (Closed). Omit to return all states.

sysparm_query.description: ServiceNow encoded query string for advanced filtering (e.g., "short_descriptionLIKEnetwork^priority=1"). Combined with individual filters (state, priority, assignment_group) when both are provided. Avoid including '&' or '=' characters that could inject additional query parameters into the URL.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of incidents to return. Defaults to "20".

create_servicenow_incident.name: create_servicenow_incident
create_servicenow_incident.description: Create a new ServiceNow incident. Requires a connected and enabled ServiceNow data source. Returns the created incident number and sys_id.

short_description.description: Brief summary of the incident (one-line title).

description.description: Detailed description of the incident. Defaults to empty if omitted.

assignment_group.description: Sys ID of the assignment group to assign the incident to.

caller_id.description: Sys ID or user name of the caller reporting the incident.

create_servicenow_change_request.name: create_servicenow_change_request
create_servicenow_change_request.description: Create a new ServiceNow change request. Requires a connected and enabled ServiceNow data source. Returns the created change request number and sys_id.

short_description.description: Brief summary of the change request (one-line title).

description.description: Detailed description of the change, including justification and plan.

assignment_group.description: Sys ID of the assignment group for the change request.

start_date.description: Planned start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., "2025-06-15T10:00:00Z").

end_date.description: Planned end date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., "2025-06-15T12:00:00Z").

add_servicenow_work_note.name: add_servicenow_work_note
add_servicenow_work_note.description: Add a work note to an existing ServiceNow record (incident, change request, or problem). Requires a connected and enabled ServiceNow data source. Work notes are internal comments visible only to IT staff.

table.description: The ServiceNow table name. Must be one of: "incident", "change_request", "problem". Other table names are not supported.

sys_id.description: The sys_id of the record to add the work note to.

work_notes.description: The work note content to add to the record.

search_concepts.name: search_concepts
search_concepts.description: Search for semantic concepts across all domains by keyword. Finds concepts by name, description, or alternate names used in different repos. Use this to find cross-repo equivalents for a field or configuration option.

query.description: Search query - can be a concept name (e.g., "timeout"), field name (e.g., "windowMs"), or description keyword (e.g., "rate limit duration").

limit.description: Maximum number of concepts to return (default 15).

list_semantic_domains.name: list_semantic_domains
list_semantic_domains.description: List all semantic domains discovered across repositories. Domains are high-level categories of related functionality (e.g., "rate-limiting", "authentication", "caching"). Use this to understand what cross-cutting concepts exist in your codebase.

minConceptCount.description: Minimum number of concepts a domain must have to be included. Default is 1.

minRepositoryCount.description: Minimum number of repositories implementing the domain. Default is 1.

limit.description: Maximum number of domains to return (default 20).

get_domain_concepts.name: get_domain_concepts
get_domain_concepts.description: Get all semantic concepts within a domain. Shows concepts with their descriptions, implementation counts, and the various names used across different repositories. Use this to understand what abstractions exist within a domain like "rate-limiting" or "authentication".

domain.description: Domain name to get concepts for (e.g., "rate-limiting", "authentication"). Supports partial matching.

limit.description: Maximum number of concepts to return (default 30).

get_concept_implementations.name: get_concept_implementations
get_concept_implementations.description: Get all implementations of a semantic concept across repositories. Shows how the same abstract concept is implemented with different field names, types, and interfaces in different codebases. Use this for migration guidance or to understand cross-repo field equivalents.

concept.description: Concept name to get implementations for (e.g., "time-window", "max-requests"). Supports partial matching.

repository.description: Filter to implementations in a specific repository.

limit.description: Maximum number of implementations to return (default 20).

find_equivalent_fields.name: find_equivalent_fields
find_equivalent_fields.description:
Find equivalent fields across repositories for a given field name. Given a field like "windowMs" from one repo, finds semantically equivalent fields in other repos (e.g., "timeWindow", "duration", "Period").
PREFER: Use 'code_migration' for complete migration guidance including field mappings, migration examples, and ADR context in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the field equivalents without the full migration context, or when exploring semantic patterns across repos.

fieldName.description: Field name to find equivalents for (e.g., "windowMs", "maxRetries", "timeout").

sourceRepository.description: Repository the field comes from (helps narrow down the concept).

limit.description: Maximum number of equivalent fields to return (default 15).

resolve_pagerduty_incident.name: resolve_pagerduty_incident
resolve_pagerduty_incident.description: Resolve a PagerDuty incident. Requires a connected and enabled PagerDuty data source. The requesterEmail must match a valid PagerDuty user. Optionally include a resolution note.

incidentId.description: The PagerDuty incident ID to resolve (e.g., "P1234567").

requesterEmail.description: Email address of the user resolving the incident. Must be a valid PagerDuty user email.

resolution.description: Optional resolution summary describing how the incident was resolved.

add_pagerduty_note.name: add_pagerduty_note
add_pagerduty_note.description: Add a note to an existing PagerDuty incident. Requires a connected and enabled PagerDuty data source. Notes are visible on the incident timeline.

incidentId.description: The PagerDuty incident ID to add a note to (e.g., "P1234567").

content.description: The note content to add to the incident.

requesterEmail.description: Email address of the user adding the note. Must be a valid PagerDuty user email.

acknowledge_pagerduty_incident.name: acknowledge_pagerduty_incident
acknowledge_pagerduty_incident.description: Acknowledge a PagerDuty incident. Requires a connected and enabled PagerDuty data source. The requesterEmail must match a valid PagerDuty user.

incidentId.description: The PagerDuty incident ID to acknowledge (e.g., "P1234567").

requesterEmail.description: Email address of the user acknowledging the incident. Must be a valid PagerDuty user email.

get_team_services.name: get_team_services
get_team_services.description: Get all services and packages owned by a team. Returns services with their tier, packages maintained, and team contacts. Use this to understand a team's scope or find services by team.

teamName.description: Team name (e.g., "Commerce Team", "Platform", "Payments"). Supports partial matching.

get_service_ownership.name: get_service_ownership
get_service_ownership.description:
Get comprehensive ownership and contact information for a service. Returns the owning team, Slack channel, oncall contact, PagerDuty escalation, service tier, compliance requirements, and related runbooks.
PREFER: Use 'incident_response' during incidents - it includes ownership plus escalation paths, runbooks, and similar past incidents in one call. Use 'investigate_service' for general investigation - includes ownership context.
USE THIS WHEN: You specifically need ownership/contact info without incident context, or when building team directory lookups.

serviceName.description: Service name to look up (e.g., "order-service" or "payment-service"). Supports partial matching.

get_incident_contacts.name: get_incident_contacts
get_incident_contacts.description:
Get escalation contacts for an incident affecting a service or feature. Returns the primary team to contact, escalation paths, related runbooks, and past incidents for context.
PREFER: Use 'incident_response' for complete incident support - includes contacts, runbooks, past incidents, and ownership details in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need only the contact/escalation info, or when you already have runbook and incident context from other sources.

service.description: Service name that is affected (e.g., "order-service", "checkout"). Can also be a feature name like "checkout" or "payments".

get_code_reviewers.name: get_code_reviewers
get_code_reviewers.description: Get suggested code reviewers for a service or file path. Combines CODEOWNERS data with git expertise analysis to suggest the best reviewers. Returns team owners, recent contributors, and domain experts.

path.description: File path or service name to find reviewers for (e.g., "services/payment-service/" or "checkout.service.ts").

get_all_teams.name: get_all_teams
get_all_teams.description: Get all teams in the organization with their contact information and services/packages they own. Use this to understand team structure and find the right team to contact.

includeServices.description: Include list of services owned by each team. Default: true.

get_opsgenie_alert.name: get_opsgenie_alert
get_opsgenie_alert.description: Retrieve details of an Opsgenie alert. Use this to get alert context before taking actions like acknowledging or closing. Requires a connected and enabled Opsgenie data source.

alertId.description: The Opsgenie alert ID to retrieve.

escalate_opsgenie_alert.name: escalate_opsgenie_alert
escalate_opsgenie_alert.description: Escalate an Opsgenie alert to the next escalation level. Requires a connected and enabled Opsgenie data source.

alertId.description: The Opsgenie alert ID to escalate.

escalationId.description: The ID of the escalation policy to use.

user.description: Display name of the user escalating the alert.

note.description: Additional note to add while escalating the alert.

close_opsgenie_alert.name: close_opsgenie_alert
close_opsgenie_alert.description: Close an Opsgenie alert, marking it as resolved. Requires a connected and enabled Opsgenie data source.

alertId.description: The Opsgenie alert ID to close.

user.description: Display name of the user closing the alert.

note.description: Additional note to add while closing the alert.

add_opsgenie_note.name: add_opsgenie_note
add_opsgenie_note.description: Add a note to an existing Opsgenie alert for additional context. Requires a connected and enabled Opsgenie data source. Notes are visible on the alert timeline.

alertId.description: The Opsgenie alert ID to add a note to.

note.description: The note content to add to the alert.

user.description: Display name of the user adding the note.

acknowledge_opsgenie_alert.name: acknowledge_opsgenie_alert
acknowledge_opsgenie_alert.description: Acknowledge an Opsgenie alert to stop further escalations. Requires a connected and enabled Opsgenie data source.

alertId.description: The Opsgenie alert ID to acknowledge.

user.description: Display name of the user acknowledging the alert.

note.description: Additional note to add while acknowledging the alert.

update_linear_issue.name: update_linear_issue
update_linear_issue.description: Update the title and description of an existing Linear issue. Both fields are always set to the provided values — retrieve the current issue first (via list_linear_issues) to avoid overwriting fields you don't intend to change. Requires a connected and enabled Linear data source. To change status, use transition_linear_issue instead.

issueId.description: The ID of the issue to update. This is the Linear internal UUID, not the human-readable identifier like "ENG-123". Use list_linear_issues to find IDs.

title.description: The new title for the issue.

description.description: The new markdown description for the issue. Pass an empty string to clear.

transition_linear_issue.name: transition_linear_issue
transition_linear_issue.description: Transition a Linear issue to a new workflow state (e.g., "In Progress", "Done"). Requires a connected and enabled Linear data source. Use list_linear_issues to find issue IDs and inspect current states.

issueId.description: The ID of the issue to transition. This is the Linear internal UUID.

stateId.description: The ID of the target workflow state. Use the Linear UI or API to discover available state IDs for the issue's team.

list_linear_teams.name: list_linear_teams
list_linear_teams.description: List all teams in the Linear workspace. Requires a connected and enabled Linear data source. Returns team IDs, names, and keys. Useful for finding the teamId parameter needed by create_linear_issue.

list_linear_issues.name: list_linear_issues
list_linear_issues.description: List recent Linear issues ordered by last updated. Requires a connected and enabled Linear data source. Returns up to 50 issues across the workspace. Use this to find issue IDs before updating or commenting.

create_linear_issue.name: create_linear_issue
create_linear_issue.description: Create a new Linear issue. Requires a connected and enabled Linear data source. Creates an issue with a title and team. Use update_linear_issue afterwards to set priority, assignee, labels, or other optional fields. Returns the created issue identifier and URL.

teamId.description: The ID of the team to create the issue in. Use list_linear_teams to find team IDs.

description.description: Markdown description of the issue. Defaults to empty if omitted.

add_linear_comment.name: add_linear_comment
add_linear_comment.description: Add a comment to an existing Linear issue. Requires a connected and enabled Linear data source. The comment body supports markdown formatting.

issueId.description: The ID of the issue to comment on. This is the Linear internal UUID.

body.description: The comment body in markdown format.

transition_jira_issue.name: transition_jira_issue
transition_jira_issue.description: Transition a Jira issue to a new status. Requires the numeric transition ID, which can be obtained from the issue's available transitions (use get_jira_issue to find the issue, then GET /rest/api/3/issue/{issueKey}/transitions). Requires a connected and enabled Jira data source.

issueKey.description: The Jira issue key to transition (e.g., "ACME-247").

transitionId.description: The numeric transition ID (e.g., "21", "31"). Get available transitions from the Jira issue's transitions endpoint.

get_jira_issue.name: get_jira_issue
get_jira_issue.description:
Get full details of a Jira issue including status, assignee, linked PRs, affected services, epic, and sprint. Use when a user mentions a Jira issue key (e.g., ACME-247) or when you need to understand what a ticket represents before implementing it.
Returns the issue's cross-source connections: which services it affects, which PRs resolve it, what epic/sprint it belongs to, and who is assigned.

issueKey.description: The Jira issue key (e.g., ACME-247, PROJ-123). Must be an exact match.

get_jira_issue_remote_links.name: get_jira_issue_remote_links
get_jira_issue_remote_links.description: Get remote links for a Jira issue. Remote links include GitHub/GitLab PRs, branches, and commits linked via platform integrations (e.g., Jira's GitHub or GitLab app). Returns the linked resources with their URLs, titles, and relationship types. Use this to discover PR-Issue links that are not in commit messages or branch names. Requires a connected and enabled Jira data source.

issueKey.description: The Jira issue key to get remote links for (e.g., "PROJ-123").

get_jira_epic.name: get_jira_epic
get_jira_epic.description:
Get an epic's details including all child issues with status, assignees, affected services, and progress summary. Use when planning work, assessing risk of a feature initiative, or understanding what a multi-story epic involves across teams and services.
Particularly useful for sprint planning and cross-team coordination — shows which services and teams are touched by the epic's stories.

epicKey.description: The epic key (e.g., ACME-200). Searches both Epic and JiraIssue entity types to find the epic.

get_active_sprint.name: get_active_sprint
get_active_sprint.description:
Get the active sprint for a Jira project, including all sprint issues with status, assignees, affected services, and velocity metrics. Use when asking about current sprint status, what the team is working on, or checking sprint health before adding more work.
Shows in-progress vs. to-do breakdown to assess sprint capacity.

projectKey.description: The Jira project key (e.g., ACME). Returns the currently active sprint for this project.

create_jira_issue.name: create_jira_issue
create_jira_issue.description: Create a new Jira issue. Requires a connected and enabled Jira data source. Builds an Atlassian Document Format (ADF) body for the description field. Returns the created issue key and self URL. Priority defaults to the project's default; use transition_jira_issue or the Jira UI to set labels, priority, or other fields after creation.

summary.description: One-line summary / title of the issue.

issueType.description: Issue type name (e.g., "Bug", "Task", "Story", "Epic"). Must match a type configured in the target project.

description.description: Plain-text description of the issue. Will be wrapped in ADF paragraph format. Defaults to empty if omitted.

assign_jira_to_sprint.name: assign_jira_to_sprint
assign_jira_to_sprint.description: Move a Jira issue into a sprint. Uses the Jira Agile REST API. Requires the sprint ID and the issue key. Call multiple times to move several issues. Requires a connected and enabled Jira data source.

sprintId.description: The numeric sprint ID (e.g., "42"). Can be obtained from the board's sprints endpoint.

issueKey.description: The issue key to move into the sprint (e.g., "ACME-123").

add_jira_comment.name: add_jira_comment
add_jira_comment.description: Add a comment to an existing Jira issue. Requires a connected and enabled Jira data source. The comment body is wrapped in Atlassian Document Format (ADF).

issueKey.description: The Jira issue key to comment on (e.g., "ACME-247").

body.description: Plain-text comment body. Will be wrapped in ADF paragraph format.

test_cypher.name: test_cypher
test_cypher.description: Simple test of Cypher query execution

search_internal_packages.name: search_internal_packages
search_internal_packages.description: Search for internal packages in JFrog Artifactory using semantic matching. Finds packages by name, description, or functionality, and enriches results with usage statistics (how many services use each package) and security data. Use this to discover internal libraries before implementing new functionality.

query.description: Search query - describe what you're looking for (package name, functionality, or natural language description like "http client with retry" or "logging library")

ecosystem.description: Filter by package ecosystem (npm, maven, pypi). Leave empty to search all.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 10, max 50)

list_all_vulnerabilities.name: list_all_vulnerabilities
list_all_vulnerabilities.description: Diagnostic tool: List all Vulnerability entities in the knowledge graph. Returns all indexed vulnerabilities to verify entity creation worked.

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default 100)

list_all_packages.name: list_all_packages
list_all_packages.description: Diagnostic tool: List all Package entities in the knowledge graph. Returns all indexed packages to verify entity creation worked.

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default 100)

get_transitive_dependents.name: get_transitive_dependents
get_transitive_dependents.description:
Get all services and packages that depend on a package, including transitive dependencies. Shows which services would need to be rebuilt or tested if this package changes.
PREFER: Use 'blast_radius' for complete impact analysis including affected flows, teams to notify, and historical incidents in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the dependency tree without the broader impact context, or when analyzing package-level dependencies only.

packageName.description: Package name to find dependents for (e.g., "@acme/logging" or "lodash"). Supports partial matching.

maxDepth.description: Maximum depth to traverse for transitive dependencies. Default is 5. Use 1 for direct dependents only.

includePackages.description: Include package-to-package dependencies in results. Default is true. Set to false to only show services.

get_recommended_packages.name: get_recommended_packages
get_recommended_packages.description:
Find recommended internal packages for a specific capability or use case. Searches for packages marked as "recommended" that match the query. Use this before adding a new dependency to see if there's an internal package you should use instead.
PREFER: Use 'dependency_check' when evaluating a specific package - includes recommended alternatives, security status, and migration guidance in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need to discover what internal packages exist for a capability, without evaluating a specific existing package.

capability.description: What you're looking for - can be a capability (e.g., "HTTP client"), use case (e.g., "logging"), or functionality (e.g., "authentication").

ecosystem.description: Filter by package ecosystem: "npm", "maven", "pypi", "go". Leave empty to search all ecosystems.

get_package_vulnerabilities.name: get_package_vulnerabilities
get_package_vulnerabilities.description:
Get all vulnerabilities linked to a package.
PREFER: Use 'dependency_check' for complete package health assessment including vulnerabilities, license status, upgrade history, and blast radius in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the vulnerability list for a specific package, or when scanning multiple packages systematically.

packageName.description: Package name pattern to search for

get_package_usage_patterns.name: get_package_usage_patterns
get_package_usage_patterns.description: Analyze how a package is used across your organization. Shows which services use the package and usage statistics. Helpful for understanding adoption and identifying usage patterns.

get_migration_status.name: get_migration_status
get_migration_status.description:
Get the migration status for a deprecated or replaced package. Shows which services have migrated vs. still pending, with team contacts for follow-up.
PREFER: Use 'code_migration' for complete migration guidance including field mappings, migration examples, and ADR context in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the migration progress numbers without the full migration context, or when tracking multiple migrations.

newPackage.description: The recommended replacement package (e.g., "@acme/logging"). If omitted, will try to find from package metadata.

get_migration_path.name: get_migration_path
get_migration_path.description:
Get migration guidance for upgrading or replacing a package. Shows which services depend on the package and suggests alternatives.
PREFER: Use 'code_migration' for complete migration guidance including field mappings, migration examples, ADR context, and adoption status in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the dependency graph for a package without the full migration context, or when planning migrations for multiple packages.

fromPackage.description: Package name to migrate from (e.g., "moment" or "pac-resolver")

toPackage.description: Target package to migrate to. If omitted, shows current dependents only.

get_migration_examples.name: get_migration_examples
get_migration_examples.description:
Find examples of services that have migrated from one package to another. Shows which services completed the migration, when, and by whom.
PREFER: Use 'code_migration' for complete migration guidance including field mappings, ADR context, and recommended patterns in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the list of services that completed a migration, or when tracking migration progress across the organization.

toPackage.description: Package being migrated to (e.g., "@acme/logging", "@acme/http-client"). If omitted, shows all services that removed the fromPackage.

get_cve_blast_radius.name: get_cve_blast_radius
get_cve_blast_radius.description:
Get the complete TRANSITIVE blast radius for a CVE - which packages are directly affected and which services/packages transitively depend on them. Traverses up to 10 levels of dependency chains to find all impacted services.
Returns: - Affected packages (directly vulnerable) - Affected services with dependency chains showing how they're impacted - Team ownership and contact information - Separation of direct vs. transitive dependencies - Critical tier services highlighted
Example: If lodash has CVE-2020-8203, finds not only services using lodash directly, but also services using @acme/auth which depends on lodash.
PREFER: Use 'blast_radius' for broader impact analysis that includes affected flows, historical incidents, and complete team notifications.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the CVE-specific blast radius without the full incident context, or when analyzing multiple CVEs systematically.

get_adoption_status.name: get_adoption_status
get_adoption_status.description:
Get adoption status for a recommended package or migration. Shows which services have adopted the package vs. still using old alternatives.
PREFER: Use 'code_migration' for complete migration guidance including adoption status, field mappings, migration examples, and ADR context in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the adoption percentages and service lists, or when tracking progress across multiple packages.

packageName.description: The recommended package to check adoption for (e.g., "@acme/http-client").

check_dependency_health.name: check_dependency_health
check_dependency_health.description:
Check the security and license health of a package dependency. Returns vulnerabilities from JFrog Xray, license compliance status, blast radius (which services in your organization use this package), and migration recommendations if issues are found.
PREFER: Use 'dependency_check' for complete health assessment including upgrade history, migration examples, and recommended alternatives in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the security/license status without upgrade history context, or when checking multiple packages in a loop.

packageName.description: Package name to check. Format depends on ecosystem: - npm: "lodash" or "@scope/package" - maven: "org.apache.commons:commons-lang3" - pypi: "requests"

version.description: Specific version to check. If omitted, checks latest indexed version.

traverse_edges.name: traverse_edges
traverse_edges.description:
Traverse edges from an entity to discover related entities. Use this after find_entities to explore the graph structure and relationships.
Direction controls which edges to follow: - "out": Edges where this entity is the source (this -> related) - "in": Edges where this entity is the target (related -> this) - "both": Edges in either direction (default)
Common edge types: depends_on, calls, contains, manages, created_together, references, produces, consumes
Example workflow: 1. find_entities(query="payment service") -> get service ID 2. traverse_edges(entityId="...", direction="out") -> what does it depend on? 3. traverse_edges(entityId="...", direction="in") -> what depends on it? 4. traverse_edges(entityId="...", edgeType="calls") -> what does it call?
For multi-hop traversal, call this tool repeatedly with the returned entity IDs.

entityId.description: The ID of the entity to start traversal from

limit.description: Maximum number of related entities to return (default 25)

get_entity_by_id.name: get_entity_by_id
get_entity_by_id.description:
Get full details of a specific entity by its ID. Use this when you already have an entity ID (e.g., from find_entities or traverse_edges results) and need to see its complete data.
Returns: id, name, type, data (JSON properties), createdAt, createdBy

find_entities.name: find_entities
find_entities.description:
Find entities in the knowledge graph using semantic search. Use this as your starting point for graph exploration - it understands natural language queries and finds semantically similar content even without exact keyword matches.
Search strategies: - Natural language: "authentication services" finds auth-related entities - Conceptual: "security vulnerabilities" finds security findings - Filtered: Set entityTypes to narrow results (e.g., ["Service", "CodePattern"])
Common entity types: Service, CodePattern, Finding, CodeExpert, CodeHotspot, CodeCoupling, Feature, ModuleBoundary, Package, Vulnerability
Returns entity ID, type, name, data, and similarity score. Use returned IDs with traverse_edges to explore relationships or get_entity_by_id for details.

query.description: Natural language search query describing what you're looking for

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 10)

search_gitlab_issues.name: search_gitlab_issues
search_gitlab_issues.description:
Search GitLab issues in the knowledge graph. Supports filtering by service name, state, and free-text query against title/description. Use this to find open or closed GitLab issues related to a service or topic.
Prefer investigate_service when you need a full service overview — use this tool when you need a targeted GitLab issue search (e.g. all open issues for a service, issues matching a keyword).

query.description: Free-text search against issue title and description (case-insensitive substring match). Leave empty to return all issues matching other filters.

serviceName.description: Filter to issues linked to a specific service (e.g. "order-service"). Matches against the service node name using a partial, case-insensitive match.

state.description: Filter by issue state: "opened" (default) or "closed". Pass "all" to return both.

create_gitlab_merge_request.name: create_gitlab_merge_request
create_gitlab_merge_request.description: Create a new merge request in a GitLab project. Requires a connected and enabled GitLab data source. Returns the created merge request details including IID and web URL.

projectId.description: GitLab project ID (numeric) or URL-encoded path (e.g., "123" or "group%2Fproject").

sourceBranch.description: The source branch name for the merge request.

description.description: Description body of the merge request. Supports GitLab Flavored Markdown.

add_gitlab_mr_comment.name: add_gitlab_mr_comment
add_gitlab_mr_comment.description: Add a comment (note) to an existing GitLab merge request. Requires a connected and enabled GitLab data source.

projectId.description: GitLab project ID (numeric) or URL-encoded path.

mergeRequestIid.description: The internal ID (IID) of the merge request within the project.

body.description: Comment body text. Supports GitLab Flavored Markdown.

query_gcp_error_group_stats.name: query_gcp_error_group_stats
query_gcp_error_group_stats.description: Query GCP Error Reporting API for error group statistics with time-windowed counts. Returns timedCounts arrays for spike detection. Requires a connected and enabled gcp_monitoring data source.

timeRangePeriod.description: Predefined time range: PERIOD_1_HOUR, PERIOD_6_HOURS, PERIOD_1_DAY, PERIOD_1_WEEK, PERIOD_30_DAYS. Default: PERIOD_1_HOUR.

timedCountDuration.description: Duration for timed count buckets (e.g., "900s" for 15 minutes). Default: 900s.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of error groups to return. Default: 50.

list_flows.name: list_flows
list_flows.description: List all business flows discovered in the codebase. Includes both CriticalFlow (discovered from code, spanning 3+ services) and Flow (documented in markdown) entities. Use this to understand the major business processes in your system.

flowType.description: Filter by flow type: "CriticalFlow" (code-discovered) or "Flow" (documented). Leave empty for both types.

minServices.description: Minimum number of services a flow must span. Default is 1.

limit.description: Maximum number of flows to return (default 20).

get_service_flows.name: get_service_flows
get_service_flows.description:
Get all flows that involve a specific service. Shows which business processes the service participates in, its role (orchestrator or participant), and the other services it collaborates with.
PREFER: Use 'investigate_service' for comprehensive service analysis including flows, dependencies, documentation, and ownership in one call. Use 'blast_radius' when assessing impact of changes to this service.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the flow participation list without the full service context, or when mapping flows for multiple services.

serviceName.description: Service name to find flows for (e.g., "order-service", "payment-api"). Supports partial matching.

limit.description: Maximum number of flows to return (default 15).

get_flow.name: get_flow
get_flow.description:
Get detailed information about a specific flow including all steps, services involved, compensation logic, and related incidents/ADRs.
PREFER: Use 'understand_flow' for complete flow analysis including ownership, runbooks, past incidents, and architectural decisions in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the flow definition and steps without the full operational context, or when listing multiple flows.

flowName.description: Flow name to look up (e.g., "checkout", "order-processing"). Supports partial matching.

get_flow_services.name: get_flow_services
get_flow_services.description:
Get all services involved in a flow with their roles and order. Shows the orchestrator, each service's responsibility, and the sequence of service calls.
PREFER: Use 'understand_flow' for complete flow analysis including ownership, runbooks, past incidents, and architectural decisions in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need just the service participation list without the full operational context, or when comparing services across multiple flows.

flowName.description: Flow name to get services for (e.g., "checkout", "payment-processing"). Supports partial matching.

search_all_documentation.name: search_all_documentation
search_all_documentation.description: Unified search across all documentation types - incidents, ADRs, runbooks, flows, and security patterns. Returns results from all doc types ranked by relevance. Use this when you need to find any documentation related to a topic.

query.description: Search query - can be a keyword, service name, error message, or topic. Searches across titles, descriptions, and content of all doc types.

docTypes.description: Comma-separated list of doc types to include. Options: "Incident,ADR,Runbook,Flow,SecurityPattern". Leave empty to search all types.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 20).

list_documentation.name: list_documentation
list_documentation.description: List all indexed documentation with optional filters. Returns incidents, ADRs, runbooks, flows, and security patterns. Use this to browse available documentation or filter by type/severity.

docType.description: Filter by documentation type: "Incident", "ADR", "Runbook", "Flow", "CriticalFlow", or "SecurityPattern". Leave empty for all types.

severity.description: Filter by severity (for incidents and security patterns): "critical", "high", "medium", "low", "SEV-1", "SEV-2", "SEV-3".

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 30).

get_service_documentation.name: get_service_documentation
get_service_documentation.description:
Get all documentation related to a specific service. Returns incidents that affected the service, runbooks for it, flows involving it, ADRs that mention it, and relevant security patterns.
PREFER: Use 'investigate_service' for comprehensive service analysis including documentation, dependencies, ownership, and architectural context in one call.
USE THIS WHEN: You need only the documentation artifacts without the full service context, or when compiling documentation for multiple services.

serviceName.description: Service name to find documentation for (e.g., "order-service", "payment-api"). Supports partial matching.

limit.description: Maximum number of results per doc type (default 5 each).

update_feature_decisions.name: update_feature_decisions
update_feature_decisions.description: Add a design decision to an existing Feature entity. Use this to record architectural choices, implementation decisions, and their rationale.

decision.description: The design decision that was made

start_new_feature.name: start_new_feature
start_new_feature.description: Register a new development feature in the Context Engine. Creates a Feature entity to track the feature lifecycle including branch, worktree, decisions, and PR info. Call this after creating the git worktree and feature branch.

featureName.description: The feature name (used as entity name)

branchName.description: The full git branch name (e.g., feature/my-feature)

merge_feature_pr.name: merge_feature_pr
merge_feature_pr.description: Mark a Feature as merged. Call this after successfully merging the PR.

featureName.description: The feature name to mark as merged

get_feature.name: get_feature
get_feature.description: Retrieve information about a Feature entity by name.

featureName.description: The feature name to look up

create_feature_pr.name: create_feature_pr
create_feature_pr.description: Update a Feature entity with Pull Request information. Call this after creating a PR via gh CLI.

resolve_file_to_service.name: resolve_file_to_service
resolve_file_to_service.description:
Quick lookup: resolve a file path to its owning service.
Lighter-weight than get_file_context - just returns the service mapping without all the decision context. Use this for fast service identification.
Falls back to heuristics if no CodeModule matches: 1. Checks if path contains a known service name 2. Looks for service.json/package.json in parent directories 3. Uses repository name as fallback

filepath.description: File path to resolve (relative to repository root).

repository.description: Repository name for disambiguation in multi-repo setups.

list_code_modules.name: list_code_modules
list_code_modules.description:
List all CodeModule entities, optionally filtered by service or repository.
Shows file pattern mappings that enable decision context lookup.

service.description: Filter to modules belonging to a specific service.

repository.description: Filter to modules in a specific repository.

limit.description: Maximum number of modules to return. Default: 50.

get_file_context.name: get_file_context
get_file_context.description:
Get decision context for a file path - the key tool for "Decision Context at Point of Coding".
Given a file path being edited, returns all relevant architectural context: - The CodeModule and Service the file belongs to - ADRs that apply to this service/module - Recent incidents that affected this area - Security patterns relevant to this code - Code experts who know this file best - Blast radius (what depends on this service) - Related runbooks for operational context
Results are scored by relevance and recency.
USE THIS WHEN: A developer or agent is about to modify a file and needs to understand the architectural context, past issues, and potential impact before making changes.
Example: get_file_context(filepath="src/payment/processor.ts") Returns: ADRs about payment idempotency, past payment incidents, PCI security patterns, experts on the payment module, services that depend on payment-service.

filepath.description: The file path being edited (relative to repository root). Examples: "src/payment/processor.ts", "packages/auth/src/index.ts"

repository.description: Repository name to scope the search. If omitted, searches across all repositories.

includeBlastRadius.description: Include blast radius analysis (services that depend on this one). Default: true. Set to false for faster response when impact analysis isn't needed.

incidentLookbackDays.description: How many days back to look for relevant incidents. Default: 180 (6 months).

create_code_module.name: create_code_module
create_code_module.description:
Register a CodeModule mapping file patterns to a service or package.
CodeModules bridge file paths to architectural entities. When a developer edits a file matching a module's patterns, the system can surface relevant ADRs, incidents, and context.
Example: Create a module for payment processing code: create_code_module(
  name="payment-processor",
  filePatterns=["src/payment/**", "lib/payment-utils/**"],
  service="payment-service",
  criticality="critical",
  tags=["pci-compliant", "financial"]
)
NOTE: This tool creates the entity. For bulk import, use the code-module-discovery agent.

name.description: Unique name for the code module (e.g., "payment-processor", "auth-middleware"). Use lowercase with hyphens.

description.description: Brief description of what this module contains and its purpose.

filePatterns.description: Glob patterns that match files belonging to this module. Examples: ["src/payment/**"], ["packages/auth/src/**", "packages/auth/lib/**"]

service.description: Name of the Service entity this module belongs to. Must match an existing Service entity name.

package.description: Name of the Package entity this module belongs to (for internal packages). Use either service OR package, not both.

criticality.description: Criticality level: "critical", "high", "medium", "low". Affects how prominently context is surfaced. Default: "medium".

tags.description: Tags for categorization and pattern matching. Examples: ["pci-compliant", "user-facing", "background-job"]

repository.description: Repository this module belongs to (for multi-repo setups).

update_confluence_page.name: update_confluence_page
update_confluence_page.description: Update an existing Confluence page's content. Requires a connected and enabled Confluence data source. You must provide the current version number (incremented by 1). Use get_confluence_page first to retrieve the current version.

pageId.description: The ID of the Confluence page to update.

title.description: The updated title of the page.

body.description: The updated page content in Confluence storage format (XHTML).

version.description: The new version number. Must be the current version + 1. Use get_confluence_page to retrieve the current version first.

search_confluence_pages.name: search_confluence_pages
search_confluence_pages.description: Search for Confluence pages using CQL (Confluence Query Language). Requires a connected and enabled Confluence data source. Example queries: 'title = "Meeting Notes"', 'space = "ENG" AND type = "page"', 'text ~ "deployment"'.

query.description: CQL query string. Examples: 'title = "Page Title"', 'space = "ENG" AND text ~ "search term"', 'label = "runbook"'.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 10.

get_confluence_page.name: get_confluence_page
get_confluence_page.description: Retrieve a Confluence page's content and metadata. Use this to get page context before updating, including the current version number needed for updates. Requires a connected and enabled Confluence data source.

pageId.description: The ID of the Confluence page to retrieve.

create_confluence_page.name: create_confluence_page
create_confluence_page.description: Create a new page in a Confluence space. Requires a connected and enabled Confluence data source. The body should be in Confluence storage format (XHTML). Returns the created page ID, title, and URL.

spaceId.description: The Confluence space ID to create the page in. This is the space's internal ID (not the human-readable key). Use search_confluence_pages with CQL 'type = "space"' to find space IDs.

title.description: The title of the new page.

body.description:
The page content in Confluence storage format (XHTML). For example: "

Hello world

" or "

Title

Content

".

parentId.description: Optional parent page ID. If provided, the new page will be created as a child of this page.

status.description: Page status. Defaults to "current" (published). Use "draft" for unpublished pages.

add_confluence_comment.name: add_confluence_comment
add_confluence_comment.description: Add a footer comment to a Confluence page. Requires a connected and enabled Confluence data source. Comments are visible at the bottom of the page.

pageId.description: The ID of the Confluence page to comment on.

body.description:
The comment content in Confluence storage format (XHTML). For example: "

This looks good!

".

understand_flow.name: understand_flow
understand_flow.description:
Deep dive into a business flow or process. Shows the complete step-by-step flow, all services involved with their roles, related architectural decisions, past incidents, and operational runbooks. Use this to understand how a feature or business process works end-to-end.
This composite skill replaces the need to call: get-flow, get-flow-services, search-adrs, search-incidents, and get-runbook individually.

flowName.description: Flow or process name (e.g., "checkout", "payment-processing", "order-fulfillment", "user-registration"). Supports partial matching.

investigate_service.name: investigate_service
investigate_service.description:
Comprehensive service investigation that returns everything you need to understand a service: its details, dependencies, dependents, ownership, documentation, business flows, active Jira issues, and GitLab issues. Use this as your FIRST tool when investigating, debugging, or understanding any service.
This composite skill replaces the need to call: get-service, get-service-dependencies, get-service-dependents, get-service-ownership, get-service-documentation, get-service-flows, and search_gitlab_issues individually.

serviceName.description: Service name to investigate (e.g., "order-service", "payment-api"). Supports partial matching.

incident_response.name: incident_response
incident_response.description:
Everything you need during an incident: escalation contacts, runbooks, similar past incidents with their root causes, and service ownership. Use this IMMEDIATELY when there's a production issue or incident.
This composite skill replaces the need to call: get-incident-contacts, get-runbook, search-incidents, get-service-ownership, and get-security-patterns individually.

service.description: Service or feature experiencing the issue (e.g., "checkout", "payment-service", "order-api"). Supports partial matching.

symptom.description: Description of the issue/symptom (e.g., "5xx errors", "high latency", "connection timeouts"). Used to find similar past incidents.

dependency_check.name: dependency_check
dependency_check.description:
Complete health check for a package dependency. Shows vulnerabilities, upgrade history (did past upgrades require code changes?), migration examples from other teams, and recommended internal alternatives. Use BEFORE adding or upgrading any dependency.
This composite skill replaces the need to call: check-dependency-health, get-package-upgrade-history, get-migration-examples, get-recommended-packages, and get-package-vulnerabilities individually.

packageName.description: Package name to check (e.g., "lodash", "@acme/http-client", "axios", "moment"). Supports partial matching.

ecosystem.description: Package ecosystem: "npm", "maven", "pypi", "go". Leave empty to search all ecosystems.

code_migration.name: code_migration
code_migration.description:
Complete migration guidance for moving from one package/pattern to another. Shows current migration status, examples from teams that have completed it, equivalent field mappings, and services still pending migration. Use when planning or executing a migration.
This composite skill replaces the need to call: get-migration-status, get-migration-examples, find-equivalent-fields, get-adoption-status, and get-transitive-dependents individually.

fromPackage.description: Package or pattern to migrate FROM (e.g., "moment", "axios", "log4js", "@old/auth"). Supports partial matching.

toPackage.description: Package or pattern to migrate TO (e.g., "date-fns", "@acme/http-client", "@acme/logging"). If omitted, will suggest alternatives.
